name: Ruins of Bobastro from El Chorro via the Tajo de la Encantada Reservoir
name_original: Ruinas de Bobastro desde El Chorro por el Embalse Tajo de la Encantada
region_intro: Hiking around Álora provides access to diverse natural landscapes within Andalusia's Guadalhorce Valley. The region is characterized by dramatic canyons such as the Desfiladero de los Gaitanes, carved by the Guadalhorce River, and features serene lakeside trails around reservoirs like the Embalse del Guadalhorce. Surrounding mountain ranges, including the Montes de Málaga Natural Park and El Hacho mountain, offer varied terrain from pine forests to panoramic viewpoints. Best hiking trails around Álora The most popular hiking route is Caminito del Rey from El Chorro, a difficult 5.4 miles (8.6 km) trail that takes 4 hours 2 minutes to complete, winding along cliff-side paths and suspended footbridges. Another top favourite among local hikers is Ruins of Bobastro from El Chorro via the Tajo de la Encantada Reservoir, a moderate 8.6 miles (13.8 km) path. This route offers views of the reservoir and historical ruins. Local hikers also love the Pared del Huma – Arabic Steps loop from El Chorro-Caminito del Rey, a difficult 9.4 miles (15.2 km) trail leading through mountainous terrain, often completed in about 5 hours 46 minutes. Hiking around Álora is defined by dramatic gorges, serene lakeside paths, and mountain ascents. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy walks to challenging climbs. The routes in Álora are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 1,300 reviews. More than 13,000 hikers have used komoot to explore Álora's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available around Álora?
A: Álora offers a wide variety of hiking opportunities, with over 60 trails to explore. These routes cater to different skill levels, ensuring there's something for everyone, from easy strolls to challenging mountain ascents.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect when hiking near Álora?
A: The terrain around Álora is incredibly diverse. You'll find dramatic canyons like the Desfiladero de los Gaitanes, serene lakeside paths around the Embalse del Guadalhorce, and mountainous areas such as El Hacho and parts of the Montes de Málaga Natural Park. Expect a mix of rocky paths, forest trails, and open landscapes with panoramic views.

Q: Are there hiking options for beginners or those looking for an easy walk?
A: Yes, Álora has plenty of options for beginners and those seeking easier walks. Approximately 25 of the trails are classified as easy, offering gentle paths suitable for a relaxed outing. For example, some routes around the town itself or over El Hacho provide pleasant walks with views of olive and almond groves.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Álora?
A: The best time to hike in Álora is typically during the spring (March to May) and autumn (September to November) months. During these periods, the weather is mild and pleasant, making for comfortable hiking conditions. Summers can be very hot, while winters are generally mild but can bring some rain.

Q: Are there any notable natural landmarks or viewpoints to discover on hikes in Álora?
A: Absolutely. The region is rich in natural landmarks. The most iconic is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3834062" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Caminito del Rey&lt;/a&gt; within the Desfiladero de los Gaitanes, offering breathtaking gorge views. Other highlights include the serene Embalse del Guadalhorce, the panoramic vistas from El Hacho mountain, and the unique limestone formations of &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/230042" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;The Torcal of Antequera&lt;/a&gt;, a short drive away.

Q: Can I find any historical sites or unique attractions along the trails?
A: Yes, several trails lead to or pass by interesting historical sites. For instance, the route &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e995629790/ruins-of-bobastro-from-el-chorro-via-the-tajo-de-la-encantada-reservoir" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ruins of Bobastro from El Chorro via the Tajo de la Encantada Reservoir&lt;/a&gt; takes you past ancient ruins. You can also explore fascinating &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6276908" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cave Houses of Sierra Parda&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4203187" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mozarabic Rock-Cut Church of Bobastro&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes available in the Álora area?
A: Yes, Álora offers several circular hiking routes. A popular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/1274905" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pared del Huma – Arabic Steps loop from El Chorro-Caminito del Rey&lt;/a&gt;, which provides a challenging and rewarding experience. Another is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10297965" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Álora Town Hall – View of Hacho loop from Álora&lt;/a&gt;, offering great views directly from the town.

Q: Are the hiking trails around Álora suitable for families with children?
A: Many trails around Álora are suitable for families, especially the easier routes. Paths around the town and some sections of El Hacho are generally accessible and offer pleasant walks without significant difficulty. Always check the specific route details for distance and elevation to ensure it matches your family's capabilities.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the hiking trails in Álora?
A: Generally, dogs are welcome on many hiking trails in the Álora region. However, specific rules may apply in protected areas or on certain popular routes like the Caminito del Rey, where dogs are typically not permitted. It's always best to check local regulations or specific trail information before heading out with your canine companion.

Q: Is public transport available to access hiking trailheads in Álora?
A: Yes, Álora is well-connected, especially for accessing the Caminito del Rey and El Chorro area. There are convenient train and bus connections from Álora to the starting points near El Chorro, making it easier to reach some of the most popular trailheads without a car.

Q: Where can I find parking for hiking trails around Álora?
A: Parking is generally available at or near popular trailheads, particularly in areas like El Chorro which serves as a gateway to the Caminito del Rey and other routes. For trails starting directly from Álora, you can often find parking within the town or at designated spots near the trail entrances. It's advisable to arrive early during peak season.

Q: What do other hikers say about their experience on the trails in Álora?
A: The hiking routes in Álora are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 1,300 reviews. Hikers often praise the dramatic landscapes, the well-maintained paths, and the variety of options available, from challenging gorge walks to serene lakeside trails.

Q: Are there opportunities for wild swimming near the hiking trails?
A: Yes, the Guadalhorce River and its associated reservoirs, such as the Embalse del Guadalhorce, offer opportunities for refreshing dips. While not always directly adjacent to every trail, these serene lakeside areas provide a chance to cool off after a hike, especially during warmer months. Always be mindful of local regulations and safety.
tips: From this bridge, you leave the village of El Chorro behind and you can see the Tajo de la Encantada reservoir that meanders until it reaches the foot of the Caminito del Rey gorge. Also from here you can see the cave that is part of the enormous Frontales climbing sector.

The viewpoint of the Mesas de Villaverde is located on the upper dam of the Tajo de la Encantada, which has a curious Y-shape due to the three hills that surround it. From here there is also an incredible view of the Gaitanes gorge and the adapted walkways.

This architectural complex is made up of a necropolis and a cave church of Mozarabic origin; it stands out not only for its surroundings but also because the church is completely carved out of the rock. It can be accessed during visiting hours, from Tuesday to Sunday, from 10:00 to 15:00. The entrance fee is 3 euros.

The only surviving resistance fighter against the Moors lived in this shelter area.
preserved are parts of the Christian rock church and individual cave parts.

waypoints.name: Entrance to the Mozarabic Ruins of Bobastro
